---
title: "Analyzing US Census Data"
author: "Alex Liu"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(tidycensus)
library(sf)
```

```{r}
#| message: false
#| cache: true
#| include: false
income_tx <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = "B19013_001",
  state = "TX",
  year = 2020,
  geometry = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
#| message: false
edu_state <- get_acs(
  geography = "state",
  variables = c("B15003_001", "B15003_022", "B15003_023", "B15003_024", "B15003_025"),
  summary_var = "B15003_001",
  year = 2020
)
```

```{r}
#| message: false
#| fig-height: 10

edu_pct <- edu_state |>
  filter(variable != "B15003_001") |>
  group_by(GEOID, NAME) |>
  summarize(
    bachelors_plus = sum(estimate),
    total = first(summary_est),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(pct_bachelors_plus = bachelors_plus / total * 100)

ggplot(edu_pct, aes(x = pct_bachelors_plus, y = fct_reorder(NAME, pct_bachelors_plus))) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Educational Attainment Varies Widely Across U.S. States",
    subtitle = "Percent of adults aged 25+ with a bachelor's degree or higher, 2020",
    x = "Percent with Bachelor's Degree or Higher",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates (2020), Table B15003"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray30", size = 11),
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "gray50", size = 8, hjust = 0),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)
  )
```
